project.key_breakthrough: diameter MAPE reached 1.96% (goal <2%) by combining blended radius formula with lower LR fine-tuning at conf=0.45.
lessons_learned: Blended formula (min(w,h)+sqrt(w*h))/2 is optimal — neither extreme handles all bubble sizes: 1.96% vs 3.88% MAPE.
Fine-tuning LR (0.002→0.0005) closed the final 0.2% gap; conf threshold sweeps to higher values as bbox quality improves.
No-catheter data (large 2-2.5um bubbles) was essential — model failed on unseen bubble sizes without it.
dead_ends: YOLO segmentation masks — upsampled 160x160 prototypes, inherently rectangular for circular objects.
sqrt(w*h)/2 radius alone overestimates ring-shaped large bubbles by ~20%.
min(w,h)/2 radius alone underestimates small bubbles — systematic bias in opposite direction.
project.trajectory: Started at ~9% MAPE with YOLO seg. Switched to detection + circle post-processing, expanded data to 30 images, refined radius formula and LR over 10 runs to hit 1.96% MAPE — beating the <2% target.
